task_completion.description: The agent focuses on or maximizes the Sagittal view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sagittal view panel (showing a side-profile of the head) is active, maximized, or being manipulated in the Blue Sky Plan interface.

task_completion.description: The rendering mode is changed to simulate a 2D X-ray projection.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image in the Sagittal view appears as a thick slab, Ray Sum, or MIP projection (looking like a traditional transparent X-ray with overlapping structures), rather than a single thin slice or a 3D surface render.

task_completion.description: The projection clearly displays both bony landmarks and the soft tissue profile.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The X-ray image shows the outline of the soft tissue (nose, lips, chin) along with the underlying bones (jaws, skull base).

task_completion.description: The agent opens a dialog to save or export the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As', 'Export Image', or similar dialog box is visible on the screen, initiated from the Blue Sky Plan software.

task_completion.description: The agent saves the image with the correct filename and path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The filename 'lateral_ceph.jpg' and the directory 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\BlueSkyPlan\' are visible in the save dialog before saving, or in a file explorer confirming the save.

integrity.description: The agent used the Blue Sky Plan GUI to adjust the view and save the image.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through Blue Sky Plan menus, view settings, and export dialogs, rather than using terminal commands to generate the image.

integrity.description: The exported image is generated from the software's view.
integrity.visual_evidence: The image being saved matches the adjusted Sagittal view in the Blue Sky Plan software, not an image downloaded from the internet or copied from elsewhere.

integrity.description: The agent didn't just create an empty or dummy file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent actually goes through the image export process in Blue Sky Plan, rather than using a terminal to run 'touch lateral_ceph.jpg' or renaming an unrelated file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output is a JPG file named 'lateral_ceph.jpg' saved to 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\BlueSkyPlan\'. The image must be a 2D projection (Ray Sum/MIP/thick slab appearance) showing true lateral orientation and visible soft tissue profile (nose, lips, chin) alongside bony landmarks. A thin slice or 3D surface render is incorrect. The minimum expected file size is 50KB.
